title: podman run: "cannot convert version" when cri-o is installed
body:
With cri-o-1.9.8-1.git7d9d2aa.fc27 installed:

    # podman run fedora date
    error parsing result from CNI plugins: cannot convert version ["" "0.1.0" "0.2.0"] to 0.3.1
    # echo $?
    127

This is podman-0.3.2-gitff9da1f.fc27.

rm'ing `/etc/cni/net.d/100-crio-bridge.conf` and `200-loopback.conf` "resolves" the problem.

Sorry for not tracking it down further; filing early because this seems kind of important.

comments.body: ed thanks for making the issue ... its a common one we are currently arguing (least I am being difficult) about
We have an incompatability with the CRI-O CNI config files right now. We need this fixed. I think this is a CRI-O issue more than an us issue, but let's keep it open here until it's resolved.
Fix merged in CRI-O. No idea when it makes it into their packaged releases, but the issue has been resolved for now.

Was there a regression? This bug is alive and well in the wild:
podman version 1.1.2
runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
spec: 1.0.1-dev
crio version 1.13.0

`Error: error parsing CNI plugin result "IP4:{IP:{IP:10.3.60.121 Mask:ffffffff} Gateway:10.3.0.1 Routes:[{Dst:{IP:10.3.0.1 Mask:ffffffff} GW:<nil>} {Dst:{IP:0.0.0.0 Mask:00000000} GW:10.3.0.1}]}, DNS:{Nameservers:[] Domain: Search:[] Options:[]}": cannot convert version ["" "0.1.0" "0.2.0"] to 0.4.0: cannot convert version ["" "0.1.0" "0.2.0"] to 0.4.0
`

Workaround: Manually injecting downgraded api version into /etc/cni/net.d/05-cilium.conf:
`+    "cniVersion": "0.3.1",`
`05-cilium.conf` isn't sourced from any of our tools; can you pastebin the contents of the file?
Also, any other files in `/etc/cni/net.d/` besides `05-cilium.conf` (and presumably our Podman config)?
